reasoning: The threshold calculated from the provided ACLED data corresponds to at least 11 fatalities in a single 30-day window. Over the past 360 days leading up to August 2025, Georgia has recorded only 1 fatality in total. While the country has recently experienced significant political upheaval and severe, brutally-policed protests (late 2024 / early 2025), these events have not involved live ammunition or mass lethal force, indicating a high threshold for state-sanctioned lethal crackdowns. Furthermore, there are no ongoing active armed conflicts producing daily casualties in the separatist regions of Abkhazia or South Ossetia. Given that no mass fatality events have occurred in August 2025, reaching 11 fatalities before the early September target dates is exceptionally unlikely. I am setting the probabilities at the minimum allowed (5%) for the nearest dates, slowly increasing to 9% by February 2026 to capture the tail risk of longer-term horizon uncertainties, such as an unexpected terrorist attack or renewed hostilities.
